index.faq.title: Frequently asked questions about the exchange rate
index.faq.q1: What is the Albanian lek exchange rate today?
index.faq.a1: The EUR/ALL, USD/ALL, GBP/ALL and CHF/ALL rates are updated daily from the European Central Bank. Check the live converter above for today's exact rate.
index.faq.q2: How do I convert Albanian lek to US dollars?
index.faq.a2: Enter the amount, select USD as the target currency and get the result instantly. The converter also works in reverse: dollars to lek.
index.faq.a3: Yes, leku.al is completely free. No registration needed, no hidden fees and no limits on conversions.
index.faq.q4: Where do the exchange rates come from?
index.faq.a4: Rates come directly from the European Central Bank (ECB) and are updated automatically every morning — the same data used by European banks as the official reference rate.

tabela.heading: Exchange rates
tabela.subtitle: Today's currency exchange rates — European Central Bank
tabela.intro: All today's exchange rates in one place: euro, US dollar, British pound and Swiss franc against the Albanian lek.
tabela.faq.title: Frequently asked questions about the rates table
tabela.faq.q1: What does the exchange rates table show?
tabela.faq.a1: The table shows the current EUR/ALL, USD/ALL, GBP/ALL and CHF/ALL rates, together with the change compared to the previous day. Data comes from the European Central Bank.
tabela.faq.a2: Rates are updated automatically every working day, based on the official European Central Bank rate published each morning.
tabela.faq.q3: Is the Albanian bank rate different?
tabela.faq.a3: Albanian banks typically apply a spread (margin) on top of the ECB rate. Our rate is the international reference — the real rate without bank commissions.

historiku.faq.title: Frequently asked questions about rate history
historiku.faq.q1: How has the euro changed against the Albanian lek this month?
historiku.faq.a1: The chart shows daily changes in the EUR/ALL rate for the last 30 days, including the minimum and maximum of the period.
historiku.faq.q2: Can I see the dollar or pound history?
historiku.faq.a2: Yes — select the currency pair from the selector: USD/ALL, GBP/ALL or CHF/ALL to view the historical chart for each currency.
historiku.faq.q3: Why does the exchange rate change every day?
historiku.faq.a3: Exchange rates change based on international trade, monetary policy and global economic conditions. Tracking history helps you find the best moment to send money.

remitanca.intro: Sending money from Italy, the UK or Greece? See exactly how many Albanian lek your family will receive, after each provider's fees.
remitanca.faq.title: Frequently asked questions about remittances to Albania
remitanca.faq.q1: Which service sends the most lek to Albania?
remitanca.faq.a1: Wise typically offers the rate closest to the official ECB rate, with fees of 0.5–1.5%. Remitly has fixed commissions that vary. Traditional banks charge a 3–5% spread without clearly disclosing it.
remitanca.faq.q2: How much does sending with Wise cost?
remitanca.faq.a2: Wise typically charges around 0.5–1.5% of the amount as a fee, with no hidden costs. Much less than traditional banks.
remitanca.faq.q3: How long does a transfer to Albania take?
remitanca.faq.a3: Wise and Remitly typically deliver within 0–2 business days. International bank transfers can take 3–5 business days.
remitanca.faq.a4: Fees are indicative, based on official data published by Wise and Remitly. The exact amount may vary slightly depending on the transfer amount, origin country and payment method.
